title: A bill to repeal the Open Meetings Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2025 enacted by the District of Columbia Council.
plain_summary: This bill cancels a law that the District of Columbia Council passed in 2025 about open meetings. The 2025 law changed some rules about when government meetings have to be open to the public. By canceling that law, this bill makes all the meeting rules go back to the way they were before 2025. This means any changes the DC Council made to open meeting requirements will no longer be in effect.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Repeal of Open Meetings Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2025
sections.text: The Open Meetings Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2025 (a law passed by the District of Columbia Council with the official code D.C. Act 26-41) is canceled and will no longer be in effect. Any other laws that were changed or removed by that Act will go back to how they were before that Act was passed. It will be as if that Act never became law in the first place.
